(Renewed) Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Regarding Its Previous Petition To Compel Arbitration Against Deft'S Berry St Llc, Bridge Homes Inc. And Roberts-Obayashi Corp.
Off Calendar Nov-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: KEVIN CANTY, ESQ., 925.939.9933, FOR PLAINTIFF MISSION WALK HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION.
SCOTT MACKEY, ESQ., 415.262.8564, FOR DEFENDANTS BERRY STREET, LLC AND BRIDGE HOMES, INC.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MISSION WALK HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION's (Renewed) Motion For Reconsideration Regarding Its Previous Petition To Compel Arbitration Against Defendants Berry Street LLC, Bridge Homes Inc. And Roberts-Obayashi Corp. is denied. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate that the opt-out provision is unconscionable. Torrance is a party in this case and the opt-out provision insures that there will not be inconsistent rulings and duplicative discovery. Even though plaintiff dismissed Torrance, the same harm (possibility of inconsistent rulings and duplicative discovery) still exists. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANTS BERRY STREET, LLC, BRIDGE HOMES, INC. AND ROBERTS-OBAYASHI CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR AS A DUPLICATE ENTRY.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-07-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KEVIN C. CANTY OF ANGIUS & TERRY LLP, TEL. NO. (925) 939-9933 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JONATHAN KEPKO OF LEWIS BRISBOIS & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 438-6646 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BERRY STREET, LLC, BRIDGE HOMES, INCORPORATED AND ROBERTS-OBAYASHI CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege each of the 2nd - 6th causes of action. Plaintiff's common law claims are not barred by the Right to Repair Act. (Liberty Mut. Ins. Co. v. Brookfield Crystal Cove (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 98.) Plaintiff has sufficiently pled the legal effect of each contract. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 (302/EHG)
